blocks.text: Led 0→1 development of an onboarding automation MVP for early-stage startups, standardizing role-based onboarding workflows, reducing manual coordination, and improving activation across Harmonize's HR product suite.
Problem
Startups needed a repeatable onboarding system that stayed flexible as teams changed.
Manual onboarding created missed steps, inconsistent employee experiences, and heavy founder/operator coordination.
Existing HR onboarding tools were too rigid for small, fast-changing teams.
Startups needed role-specific workflows without the overhead of configuring a large HRIS.
Harmonize needed a stronger activation path from free users into paid HR products.
My Role
As Lead Product Manager, I owned MVP strategy, user research, workflow design, and cross-functional execution from concept to launch.
Solution
Built a lightweight onboarding automation product that helped startup operators create role-specific onboarding workflows, schedule communications, and track completion.
Execution / Product Decisions
Defined the MVP around the highest-frequency onboarding jobs: assigning tasks, sending reminders, sharing role-specific resources, and tracking completion.
Designed configurable onboarding templates by role so teams could standardize repeatable steps while preserving flexibility.
Added automated email scheduling and reminders to reduce manual coordination for founders and HR operators.
Prioritized lightweight workflow automation over complex HRIS-style configuration to accelerate adoption and reduce setup friction.
Conducted 20+ usability interviews with startup operators and hiring managers to identify onboarding pain points and adoption blockers.
Used feedback loops and lightweight experiments to refine positioning, template design, and onboarding task flows.
Tradeoffs & Product Decisions
Standardization vs flexibility: Chose role-based templates instead of fully custom workflows so startups could launch quickly while still adapting onboarding by function.
Speed vs completeness: Prioritized the core onboarding loop over advanced HRIS features to validate demand and reduce MVP complexity.
Automation vs human control: Automated reminders and scheduled communication while keeping operators in control of final content and sequencing.
Free-to-paid conversion: Designed the MVP to create clear value during onboarding while exposing users to adjacent paid HR workflows.
Results & Impact
Increased outreach response rates from 10% to 35% by refining product positioning around startup onboarding pain points.
Increased monthly recurring revenue by 7% by improving conversion from free users into paid HR product adoption.
Reduced manual onboarding coordination through automated templates, reminders, and scheduled communication.
Improved consistency of new-hire onboarding by standardizing repeatable role-based workflows.
Created a reusable onboarding workflow foundation supporting broader HR suite engagement.
Metrics Tracked
Onboarding completion rate
Time to onboarding completion
Number of startups activating onboarding workflows
Free-to-paid conversion rate
Outreach response rate
Engagement with adjacent Harmonize HR products
Key Learnings
For early-stage teams, the best onboarding product is not the most configurable one — it is the one that helps teams get started quickly while still supporting lightweight customization.
Workflow automation works best when it reduces coordination burden without removing human judgment.
Positioning mattered as much as functionality: reframing the product around startup onboarding consistency improved response rates from 10% to 35%.
MVP success depended on narrowing the first use case, validating the core loop, and avoiding enterprise HR complexity too early.
Visual Diagrams
Before vs After Onboarding Flow — Before: founder manually sends tasks, reminders, docs, and follow-ups. After: role template → automated task list → scheduled reminders → completion tracking.
Workflow Automation System — Admin selects role template → system generates checklist → reminders trigger by schedule/status → employee completes tasks → admin tracks completion.
Product Decision Matrix — Decision | Why it mattered | Tradeoff | Outcome
